description: World's dominant manufacturer of precision cutting, grinding, and polishing equipment for semiconductors. ~70-80% global share in dicing saws, ~80% in grinders, and pioneered Stealth Dicing (laser-based, kerf-loss-free) technology with Hamamatsu Photonics. Core products: dicing saws (DFD/DAD series), laser saws (DFL series using Hamamatsu SD engine), surface/backside grinders (DFG/DAG series), surface planarizers, and precision processing tools (blades, wheels — consumables with razor-and-blade recurring revenue). Also manufactures dry polishers and KABRA plasma dicing systems. Revenue split: ~60% equipment, ~40% precision tools (consumables). FY ending March. FY2024 (Mar 2025): revenue 393.3B JPY (+27.9% YoY), OPM 42.4%, NI 123.9B JPY (+47.1%), ROE 27.6%. FY2025 guidance: revenue 419.0B JPY (+6.5%). China is 35.9% of net sales (#1 market). Founded 1937 in Hiroshima. ~5,256 employees. Unique 'Will' internal market system for resource allocation. 100% share of HBM wafer thinning grinders. Gohara Plant (Hiroshima): 14x capacity boost by 2035 for precision tools.

majorClients: TSMC (CMP-0017) — dicing, grinding, polishing; largest customer; Kumamoto JASM proximity
Samsung Electronics (CMP-0018) — dicing saws/grinders for logic & memory fabs
SK Hynix (CMP-0020) — dicing, grinding; 100% HBM wafer thinning grinders
JCET Group (CMP-0065) — dicing saws, blades (China OSAT #1)
Tongfu Microelectronics (CMP-0067) — dicing saws, blades (China OSAT #2)
Tianshui Huatian (CMP-0068) — dicing saws, blades (China OSAT #3)
Powertech Technology (CMP-0069) — dicing saws (Taiwan memory OSAT)
Hua Hong Semiconductor (CMP-0070) — dicing saws (China foundry #2)
GTA Semiconductor (CMP-0095) — dicing saws (Shanghai specialty foundry)
majorSuppliers: Hamamatsu Photonics (6965.T, CMP-0115) — Stealth Dicing (SD) engine: modularized laser + optics module for DFL laser saw series. Hamamatsu holds the SD patent portfolio; DISCO integrates the engine into its equipment platform. Deep co-development relationship.
Tomei Diamond Corporation (private) — likely supplier of synthetic diamond powder for blade/wheel manufacturing (unconfirmed)
competitors: Tokyo Seimitsu / ACCRETECH (CMP-0001) — world #2 in dicing (~10% global share vs DISCO's 70-80%); also competes in grinding/polishing
Guangli Technology / ADT (CMP-0074) — world #3 in dicing; China/Israel; growing in China domestic market
EO Technics (CMP-0099) — laser stealth dicing (Korea); alternative technology to blade dicing
Heyan Technology (CMP-0089) — first mass-produced domestic Chinese dicer; growing share in China
CETC / Shuoke Jingwei (CMP-0091) — CMP + dicing (China state-owned, CETC subsidiary)
Applied Micro (ADT subsidiary, now Guangli) — historical competitor in precision dicing

FY2024_ended_Mar2025.note: Record revenue and profit; driven by AI/HBM demand and advanced packaging growth

FY2025_9M_Apr_Dec2025.note: Continued strong demand; record-high shipments in Oct-Dec quarter

dividendHistory.policy: Performance-linked: 25% of half-yearly NI; plus additional dividend (1/3 surplus funds) on final. Minimum guarantee: 20 JPY/year.

capacityExpansion: Gohara Plant (Kure City, Hiroshima): New precision tools factory. Three-phase construction. 218,539 m2 site. Will boost tool (blades/wheels) output capacity 14x by 2035. Announced April 2025. Consolidates production from Kure and Kuwabata Plants. Also addresses BCM (tsunami risk at current Kure Plant).
Nagano Works: Existing equipment manufacturing hub; ongoing expansion for dicing saw and grinder production.
Hiroshima Works (Kure): Current main production facility for precision tools; to be gradually consolidated into Gohara.
Global service network: 90+ countries served; local service centers expanding in Taiwan, Korea, China, Southeast Asia.
keyRisks: Extreme valuation: TTM P/E 61.4x, P/B 14.8x — priced for perfection; any growth deceleration could cause sharp correction
China concentration: 35.9% of revenue; geopolitical tensions, export controls could restrict sales; domestic substitution accelerating (Heyan, Guangli)
Cyclicality: Despite premium positioning, semiconductor equipment demand is cyclical; FY2025 growth already decelerating to +6.5% from +27.9%
AI demand sustainability: Revenue growth heavily linked to AI/HBM buildout; any slowdown in AI capex would directly impact
Technology disruption: Laser dicing (EO Technics), plasma dicing (SPTS/others), and Chinese alternatives could erode blade dicing dominance long-term
FX sensitivity: ~88% overseas revenue; yen appreciation would compress margins
Single-segment risk: Pure-play on cutting/grinding — no diversification hedge unlike Tokyo Seimitsu (probing + metrology)
Founder family influence: Sekiya family holds ~9.8% combined; succession risk
growthDrivers: AI/HBM demand: 100% share in HBM wafer thinning grinders; every HBM stack requires DISCO grinding
Advanced packaging: Chiplet architectures, fan-out, 2.5D/3D packaging drive increased dicing and grinding steps per wafer
Stealth Dicing adoption: Kerf-loss-free laser dicing expanding into MEMS, memory, power devices
Consumables flywheel: ~40% of revenue from blades/wheels; installed base of 30,000+ machines globally creates annuity-like recurring revenue
Gohara Plant: 14x capacity for tools by 2035 ensures supply can meet long-term demand growth
Geographic expansion: Service network in 90+ countries; co-locating with TSMC/Samsung/Intel new fabs
KABRA plasma dicing: Next-generation dicing for ultra-thin and small chips; potential new growth vector

TokyoSeimitsu_7729T.note: Direct competitor; much lower multiples but lower margins (20% vs 42%)

valuationContext.note: DISCO commands the highest multiples in Japanese semiconductor equipment, reflecting its near-monopoly in dicing (70-80%) and unmatched margins (42%). Premium is extreme but historically justified by consistent execution. Forward P/E of 46x with only 6.5% revenue growth suggests limited margin of safety.

_metadata.dataSource: DISCO IR (disco.co.jp), Yahoo Finance, MarketScreener, StockAnalysis, Investing.com, BigGo Finance, Wisesheets, Dividend Japan
_metadata.notes: Deep research update 2026-02-23. FY2024 record results: revenue 393.3B (+27.9%), OPM 42.4%, NI 123.9B (+47.1%), ROE 27.6%. FY2025 guidance revised up: revenue 419.0B, OP 172.1B. Stock near 52-week high at 74,060 JPY; TTM P/E 61.4x, P/B 14.8x. China 35.9% of sales. Gohara Plant: 14x tool capacity by 2035. 34 identified clients across 8 countries. Hamamatsu Photonics confirmed as key supplier (SD engine). Dividend FY2025E: 437 JPY.
